patient_information.patient_address: 18 N Ontario St, New York, NY 21022

clinical_information.diagnosis: High-grade osteosarcoma of the distal femur (post surgical resection)
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy (Doxorubicin 75 mg/m2 q21d, Cisplatin 120 mg/m2 q21d, Methotrexate 12 g/m2 q14d) given March–April 2024 (3 cycles). Limb-sparing surgical resection of the distal femur with prosthetic implant on May 1, 2024. Adjuvant chemotherapy May–September 2024 (6 cycles) with reduced-dose Doxorubicin 37.5 mg/m2 q21d, Cisplatin 90 mg/m2 q21d, Methotrexate 12 g/m2 q14d, and Ifosfamide 9 g/m2 q21d with Mesna. No imaging response or post-treatment tumor status documented in the note.
clinical_information.lab_summary: Key abnormalities: pancytopenia with leukopenia (WBC 2.5 x10^9/L), anemia (Hgb 9.5 g/dL) and thrombocytopenia (platelets 75 x10^9/L). Hypoalbuminemia (albumin 3.0 g/dL). Liver tests are abnormal with mild–moderate transaminase elevation (AST 55 U/L, ALT 60 U/L) and elevated alkaline phosphatase (ALP 150 U/L), suggesting mixed hepatocellular and cholestatic injury. Markedly elevated LDH (450 U/L). Hyperferritinemia (ferritin 800 ng/mL), which can reflect inflammation, iron overload, or macrophage activation/HLH. Electrolytes, renal function, glucose, and coagulation studies are within normal limits. Recommended next steps: peripheral smear and reticulocyte count, hemolysis labs (bilirubin, haptoglobin), inflammatory markers, viral serologies (hepatitis, HIV), formal iron studies (TIBC/transferrin), abdominal imaging for hepatosplenomegaly, and consideration of bone marrow evaluation based on clinical context.
clinical_information.imaging_results: CT chest (09/04/24, noncontrast): 1.5 cm rounded, spiculated lesion in the left lower lobe; multiple additional small pulmonary nodules up to 5 mm in both lungs; no significant mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy; no pleural effusion or pleural thickening. MRI left femur (09/04/24): new irregular mass at prior resection site measuring ~4.0 cm with heterogeneous signal and T2 hyperintense areas consistent with edema/possible necrosis; tumor extends into surrounding soft tissues with irregular margins and adjacent muscle edema; extensive bone marrow edema surrounding the lesion; thickening of surrounding soft tissues consistent with desmoplastic reaction; no new suspicious lesions in the proximal femur or surrounding structures. X‑ray left knee (AP and lateral, 03/01/24): aggressive-appearing lytic lesion in the distal femur ~5 cm with cortical destruction and soft tissue mass extending into the knee joint; periosteal reaction with sunburst pattern; surrounding soft tissues thickened; no significant joint effusion; no evidence of metastatic disease in surrounding bones or soft tissues. MRI findings listed for 03/02/24: no details provided.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient reports left thigh pain at the site of prior resection, and 2 weeks of non-productive cough and fatigue. Severity not quantified; symptoms are symptomatic but no documented functional limitations or systemic signs (no fever, no dyspnea).
